name: Potential Windows Scheduled Task Creation
notes: This rule triggers when adversaries attempts to create a scheduled task.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Scheduled Task Write to System32 Tasks
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Scheduled Task Write to System32 Tasks, BB:BehaviorDefinition: TaskCache Registry Modification, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Scheduled Task/Job - Windows
TD_list: APPLY Potential Windows Scheduled Task Creation on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Scheduled Task Write to System32 Tasks, BB:BehaviorDefinition: TaskCache Registry Modification, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Scheduled Task/Job - Windows
RED_list: Event name: Potential Windows Scheduled Task Creation
Event description: Detects when adversaries attempts to create a scheduled task.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
